title: Tags & Badges
intro: BBDTag, DeltaTag, and ManyTag cover status, movement, and multiplicity — every short categorical label in a Gravitate portal is one of these three. All render antd Tags underneath, so portal theming carries straight through; BBDTag alone forwards extra antd Tag props — DeltaTag and ManyTag accept only their own.

blocks.body:
Three components split the tagging job. `BBDTag` is the workhorse status/category pill — an antd Tag with Excalibrr's boolean theme props — used in grids, drawers, and page headers. `DeltaTag` colors a numeric value's movement against a threshold: green above, red below, flippable for metrics where rising is bad. `ManyTag` lays out a string array as neutral tags and collapses overflow behind a "+ N more" tooltip button.

Reach for a tag when the value is short, categorical, and scannable. Sentence-length content belongs in `Texto`; anything the user clicks to act belongs in a button. Inside GraviGrid cells, prefer the `TagCell` renderer (see Cell Renderers) over mounting BBDTags by hand.

blocks.title: BBDTag — brand and status variants
blocks.caption: The seven boolean color variants in the demo theme. Brand: theme1 (light info tint), theme2 (filled with the portal primary), theme3 (volcano), theme4 (purple). Status: success, warning, error. Omit every boolean to get the neutral default tag.

blocks.title: BBDTag props
blocks.intro: Verified against @gravitate-js/excalibrr 5.2.x. Everything not listed falls through to the underlying antd Tag (icon, bordered, closable, onClose, onClick).

rows.notes: The label — the ONLY way to set tag text. There is no text prop; a BBDTag without children renders an empty pill.
Brand palette pills. theme2 fills with the portal primary color tokens; theme1 uses the lighter info/processing tint; theme3 and theme4 map to volcano and purple presets. Set at most one.
Status presets. These imply state — reserve them for pass/fail, readiness, and severity, never for neutral metadata.
Escape hatch to any antd preset or hex; "primary" aliases theme2. An explicit color overrides every boolean except theme2.
Pass-through. Use className="text-ellipsis" with a maxWidth style for truncation. fontSize is pinned to 12px after your style spread — see gotchas.

blocks.title: BBDTag — choosing a color
blocks.intro: The color is a claim about the content. Pick by meaning, not by what looks nice next to the grid.

rows.name: default (neutral)
rows.when: Reporting attributes, groupings, counts — anything that is a fact, not a state.

rows.name: theme1 / theme2
rows.when: Brand-tinted category pills; theme2 is the filled, highest-emphasis tag, theme1 the lighter tint.

rows.name: theme3 / theme4
rows.when: A second and third categorical axis when one brand tint isn't enough.

rows.name: success / warning / error
rows.when: True state only: published/blocked, pass/fail validation, exception severity.

rows.name: color
rows.when: One-off antd preset or hex outside the seven booleans. Last resort — it bypasses the theme.

blocks.title: BBDTag — composition patterns
blocks.caption: textTransform casing, text-ellipsis truncation on a neutral tag, removable filter tags (theme2 with a CloseOutlined in children), pass/fail validation tags with leading icons, and compact percent pills sized for grid cells.

blocks.title: DeltaTag — the five states
blocks.caption: Gain (green, caret-up), drop (red, caret-down), invertColors turning a rise red for rising-is-bad metrics, textIfEqual replacing the number when value === threshold, and prefix/postfix money formatting in decimal dollars.

blocks.title: DeltaTag props
blocks.intro: DeltaTag wraps BBDTag and owns its own color — you never set success/error on it. Both value and threshold are required.

rows.notes: The metric. Rendered via toFixed(precision) with a caret icon matching its direction.
Required — usually 0. Above is green, below is red. Omitting it compares against undefined, which is always false, so every value renders red.
Decimal places. Gravitate money is decimal dollars — precision={4} for $0.0100/gal copy.
Rendered tight around the number: prefix="$" postfix="/gal" gives $0.0125/gal.
When value === threshold, renders a success tag with this text instead of the number — "On Target" beats a green 0.
Flips the mapping for metrics where rising is bad (cost, exceptions, churn).

blocks.title: ManyTag — overflow behavior
blocks.caption: The same eight items at maxCount 1, 3, 5, and unbounded. Visible items render as neutral BBDTags; the remainder collapses into a "+ N more" button whose hover tooltip lists the hidden items.

blocks.title: ManyTag props
blocks.intro: Two props, both required. Items are plain strings — map object arrays before passing.

rows.notes: The full list. If undefined the component returns null and the cell is silently blank — the prop is tagItems, not items.
Visible tags before overflow collapses into "+ N more". 2–4 fits most grid cells and cards; the prop is maxCount, not limit.

blocks.title: Canonical usage
blocks.note: Layout via Horizontal gap={8} — never style={{ gap }}. Money copy is decimal dollars ($0.0125/gal), never cents symbols.

items.do: Pass the label as children: <BBDTag success>Published</BBDTag>
items.dont: Pass text="…" or theme="success" string props
items.why: Neither prop exists. Unknown props fall through to the DOM and the tag renders as an empty pill with no warning.

items.do: Use the default neutral BBDTag for reporting attributes and metadata
items.dont: Reuse success/warning/error because the color matches the page
items.why: Status colors imply state — a green "Region: West" reads as a passing check.

items.do: Always pass threshold to DeltaTag, usually threshold={0}
items.dont: Omit threshold or invent positive/inverted props
items.why: The comparison against undefined is always false, so every value renders red regardless of sign.

items.do: Call ManyTag with tagItems and maxCount
items.dont: Call it with items and limit
items.why: tagItems is undefined, the component returns null, and the cell is silently blank.

items.title: Neutral attributes get neutral tags
items.detail: For reporting attributes and other stateless metadata, use the default BBDTag with no boolean prop. Status colors imply state: a success-green attribute tag reads as "passing" and an error-red one as "broken". This is the established rule for reporting attributes (e.g. ManageQuoteRows).

items.title: Wrong prop names fail silently
items.detail: BBDTag with text=/theme=, DeltaTag without threshold, and ManyTag with items/limit all render empty or all-red with zero console output. The /DesignSystem/Tags showcase page itself shipped with these exact bugs — trust this reference and the .d.ts, not that page's prop strings.

items.title: BBDTag pins font-size to 12px
items.detail: The component applies fontSize: 12 after spreading your style object, so style={{ fontSize: 14 }} is silently discarded. To size content differently, wrap it in a span with its own inline size: +15.2% — a child's own font-size overrides the inherited pin. Don't use className='text-xs'; that utility class exists nowhere in this stack and silently does nothing.

items.title: Color booleans have a precedence order
items.detail: Set exactly one. If several are set: theme2 wins outright, an explicit color prop beats the rest, then theme1 > theme3 > theme4 > success > warning > error. Conditional tags should compute one boolean, e.g. success={ok} error={!ok}.

items.title: ManyTag overflow is hover-only
items.detail: "+ N more" is a small antd Button inside a Tooltip — the hidden items appear only on hover and the button has no click handler. Don't use it as a disclosure control or on touch-first surfaces; if users must act on hidden items, show them another way.
